candidates.database_description: Storm-surge and sea-level-rise hazard data showing coastal inundation areas for different return periods and future sea-level scenarios. Useful for coastal flood exposure analysis and scenario-based hazard assessment.

Building and cadastral building data from Matrikkelen, including building points (representasjonspunkt), building type, building number, current building status, municipality attributes, and identifiers for linking buildings to addresses and properties.
Flood hazard susceptibility data showing areas with potential flood risk. Suitable for first-pass flood exposure analysis, municipality-level planning, and spatial overlay with buildings and parcels.
Quick clay hazard zone data, including hazard classes, consequence classes, and risk classes for areas with potential large quick-clay landslides.

replan_trigger.error: Plan not finalized after 3 validation blocks.

args.sql: SELECT kommune.kommunenummer FROM municipalities.kommune WHERE kommune.navn ILIKE 'Kristiansand%'

SELECT bygning.bygningid, ST_AsGeoJSON(ST_Transform(bygning.geometri, 4326)) AS geom_geojson FROM buildings.bygning WHERE bygning.kommunenummer = '1170' AND ST_Intersects(bygning.geometri, (SELECT stormflohavniva.geom FROM stormflohavniva.stormflohavniva WHERE stormflohavniva.returnperiod = 200 AND stormflohavniva.kommunenummer = '1170'))

SELECT bygning.bygningid, ST_AsGeoJSON(ST_Transform(bygning.geometri, 4326)) AS geom_geojson FROM buildings.bygning WHERE bygning.kommunenummer = '1170' AND ST_Intersects(bygning.geometri, (SELECT flomaktsomrader.geom FROM flomaktsomrader.flomaktsomrader WHERE flomaktsomrader.returnperiod = 200 AND flomaktsomrader.kommunenummer = '1170'))

SELECT bygning.bygningid, ST_AsGeoJSON(ST_Transform(bygning.geometri, 4326)) AS geom_geojson FROM buildings.bygning WHERE bygning.kommunenummer = '1170' AND ST_Intersects(bygning.geometri, (SELECT stormflohavniva.geom FROM stormflohavniva.stormflohavniva WHERE stormflohavniva.returnperiod = 200 AND stormflohavniva.kommunenummer = '1170')) AND ST_Intersects(bygning.geometri, (SELECT flomaktsomrader.geom FROM flomaktsomrader.flomaktsomrader WHERE flomaktsomrader.returnperiod = 200 AND flomaktsomrader.kommunenummer = '1170')) ORDER BY bygning.bygningid ASC LIMIT 10

5.description: join buildings with storm surge areas

candidates.database_description: Storm-surge and sea-level-rise hazard data showing coastal inundation areas for different return periods and future sea-level scenarios. Useful for coastal flood exposure analysis and scenario-based hazard assessment.

Building and cadastral building data from Matrikkelen, including building points (representasjonspunkt), building type, building number, current building status, municipality attributes, and identifiers for linking buildings to addresses and properties.
Flood hazard susceptibility data showing areas with potential flood risk. Suitable for first-pass flood exposure analysis, municipality-level planning, and spatial overlay with buildings and parcels.
Quick clay hazard zone data, including hazard classes, consequence classes, and risk classes for areas with potential large quick-clay landslides.

7.description: join storm-surge and flood zone results

candidates.database_description: Storm-surge and sea-level-rise hazard data showing coastal inundation areas for different return periods and future sea-level scenarios. Useful for coastal flood exposure analysis and scenario-based hazard assessment.

Building and cadastral building data from Matrikkelen, including building points (representasjonspunkt), building type, building number, current building status, municipality attributes, and identifiers for linking buildings to addresses and properties.
Flood hazard susceptibility data showing areas with potential flood risk. Suitable for first-pass flood exposure analysis, municipality-level planning, and spatial overlay with buildings and parcels.
Quick clay hazard zone data, including hazard classes, consequence classes, and risk classes for areas with potential large quick-clay landslides.
